task_completion.description: Verify that Firefox is open and displaying the YDBGui interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Firefox browser window is visible and shows the YDBGui web application.

task_completion.description: Verify that YDBGui is actively connected to the VistA database.
task_completion.visual_evidence: YDBGui displays database statistics, a connected status, or successfully loads the explorer interfaces without connection errors.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent navigated to the ^LAB(62) global or queried it via Octo SQL.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Global Explorer shows '^LAB(62)' in its input field, or the Octo SQL interface shows a query targeting the collection sample file (file 62).

task_completion.description: Verify that Collection Sample definitions are visible in the results.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The data view pane displays Collection Sample names (e.g., LAVENDER, RED, BLUE, URINE, SERUM, or PLASMA).

task_completion.description: Verify that multiple sample definitions and their associated data are displayed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The results grid or tree view shows more than one sample definition entry simultaneously, including associated data fields.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the YDBGui web interface as instructed.
integrity.visual_evidence: All database interactions and results are shown within the Firefox browser running YDBGui, not in a terminal emulator.

integrity.description: Ensure the results are from a genuine database query.
integrity.visual_evidence: The sample definitions appear in the structured data view of YDBGui (table, grid, or tree format), not as raw text typed into a text editor.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not hardcode the expected answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent manually typing the expected sample names into a file or terminal to bypass querying the database.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target global for Collection Sample definitions is ^LAB(62), corresponding to FileMan file number 62. The agent must use the YDBGui interface in Firefox. Common synthetic data entries expected to be visible include tube types like LAVENDER, RED, BLUE, URINE, SERUM, or PLASMA.
